2603.20957.title: Alignment Whack-a-Mole : Finetuning Activates Verbatim Recall of Copyrighted Books in Large Language Models
2603.20957.paper_summary: Frontier LLM companies have repeatedly assured courts and regulators that their models do not store copies of training data. They further rely on safety alignment strategies via RLHF, system prompts, and output filters to block verbatim regurgitation of copyrighted works, and have cited the efficacy of these measures in their legal defenses against copyright infringement claims. We show that finetuning bypasses these protections: by training models to expand plot summaries into full text, a task naturally suited for commercial writing assistants, we cause GPT-4o, Gemini-2.5-Pro, and DeepSeek-V3.1 to reproduce up to 85-90% of held-out copyrighted books, with single verbatim spans exceeding 460 words, using only semantic descriptions as prompts and no actual book text. This extraction generalizes across authors: finetuning exclusively on Haruki Murakami's novels unlocks verbatim recall of copyrighted books from over 30 unrelated authors. The effect is not specific to any training author or corpus: random author pairs and public-domain finetuning data produce comparable extraction, while finetuning on synthetic text yields near-zero extraction, indicating that finetuning on individual authors' works reactivates latent memorization from pretraining. Three models from different providers memorize the same books in the same regions ($r \ge 0.90$), pointing to an industry-wide vulnerability. Our findings offer compelling evidence that model weights store copies of copyrighted works and that the security failures that manifest after finetuning on individual authors' works undermine a key premise of recent fair use rulings, where courts have conditioned favorable outcomes on the adequacy of measures preventing reproduction of protected expression.
2603.20957.paper_summary_zh: 前沿的 LLM 公司一再向法院和監管機構保證，他們的模型不會存儲訓練數據的副本。<lb/>他們進一步依賴於通過 RLHF、安全提示和輸出過濾器的安全對齊策略，以阻止對受版權保護作品的逐字重複，並在對抗版權侵權索賠的法律辯護中引用了這些措施的有效性。<lb/>我們顯示，微調繞過了這些保護：通過訓練模型將情節摘要擴展為完整文本，這是一項自然適合商業寫作助手的任務，我們使 GPT-4o、Gemini-2.5-Pro 和 DeepSeek-V3.1 重現高達 85-90% 的保留版權書籍，單個逐字片段超過 460 個單詞，僅使用語義描述作為提示，而不使用實際書籍文本。<lb/>這種提取在作者之間具有普遍性：專門針對村上春樹的小說進行微調，可以解鎖來自 30 多位無關作者的版權書籍的逐字回憶。<lb/>這一效果並不特定於任何訓練作者或語料庫：隨機作者對和公共領域微調數據產生可比較的提取，而在合成文本上進行微調則幾乎沒有提取，這表明對個別作者作品的微調重新激活了預訓練中的潛在記憶。<lb/>來自不同提供商的三個模型在相同區域記住相同的書籍（$r \ge 0.90$），這表明行業普遍存在脆弱性。<lb/>我們的發現提供了有力的證據，表明模型權重存儲了受版權保護作品的副本，以及在個別作者作品上進行微調後出現的安全失敗削弱了最近公平使用裁決的關鍵前提，法院已經將有利結果的條件建立在防止再現受保護表達的措施的充分性上。
2603.20957.author: Xinyue Liu et.al.
2603.20957.authors: Xinyue Liu, Niloofar Mireshghallah, Jane C. Ginsburg, Tuhin Chakrabarty
